title: Hippuric Acid—A Better Reagent for Quantitative Estimation of Zirconium
author: SURESH KUMAR BAJPAI
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Chemistry Department, Lucknow University, U.P. 

  Manuscript received  16 January 1976;  accepted  5 April 1975 

 Hippuric acid has been found to be a better suited reagent for gravimetric estimation of zirconium. Even microquantity i.e., 1--2 mg. of zirconium can be estimated accurately in pH range 4.-5.2. Because of variable composition the precipitate is ignited and zirconium is weighed as ZrO 2 , Ba 2+ , Ca 2+ , Sr 2+ , Mg 2+ , Pb 2+ , Co 2+ , Ni 2+  and Fe 3+  are removed by single precipitation. Ti 4+ , Uo 2  2+ , AI 3+  and trivalent cerite earth ions are removed by double precipitation. Zn 2+ , Be 2+ , CO 3+ , V 2 O 4  2+  ions interfere.
